statement: Project memory lives in `.neuroflow/` in the user's repo, not in the plugin
reasoning: Config must be per-project; plugin is stateless
Context window efficiency — agents read index first, load files only when needed
Makes dependencies explicit; sentinel-dev can validate consistency
Sessions are local continuity; decisions are team-shared history

statement: `/literature` is not a standalone command — literature lives under `/ideation` via scholar agent
reasoning: Avoids redundant entry points; scholar agent is reusable across commands
Consistent naming: verb or noun, no hyphens in verb phrases

statement: Renamed `references/` folder to `reasoning/` and migrated decisions tracking to structured per-phase JSON files
reasoning: Makes decisions machine-readable with explicit statement/source/reasoning fields; per-phase structure enables granular tracking of what was decided and why at each research stage
